title_en: England needs fewer council homes, not more
title_zh: 英格兰需要的是更少的市政住房，而不是更多
rubric_en: Andy Burnham’s plan is no way to ease the housing crisis
rubric_zh: 安迪·伯纳姆的方案并不能缓解住房危机

paragraphs.en: Young Britons are a gloomy bunch. One poll found that 38% expect to lead worse lives than their parents (only 36% think they will do better). Much of the blame can be laid at the locked door of unaffordable housing. In big cities, where the good jobs are, rents are extortionate. In London the average is around half of mean earnings. Anger about housing frays the social contract and leads some to scapegoat vulnerable groups, such as refugees.
paragraphs.zh: 年轻的英国人普遍情绪低落。一项民调发现，38%的人预期自己的生活会比父母更糟（只有36%认为自己会过得更好）。许多问题都可以归咎于高得离谱且负担不起的住房之门紧紧关闭。在就业机会集中的大城市，租金高得令人发指。在伦敦，平均租金约占平均工资的一半。住房问题引发的愤怒正在撕裂社会契约，并促使一些人把责任推给弱势群体，例如难民。

paragraphs.en: Andy Burnham, the man almost certain to be the next prime minister, has an answer. He has promised “the biggest council-housebuilding programme since the post-war period”. This policy is popular: 72% of the public support more social housing, which in England means homes owned by councils or charities and let at below-market rents. Over 1.3m households are on waiting lists for such homes.
paragraphs.zh: 几乎可以肯定将成为下任首相的安迪·伯纳姆给出了一个答案。他承诺要推出“自战后以来最大的市政住房建设计划”。这一政策很受欢迎：72%的公众支持增加更多社会住房，而在英格兰，这类住房通常由地方议会或慈善机构持有，并以低于市场水平的租金出租。超过130万户家庭正在等待这样的住房。

paragraphs.en: Yet Mr Burnham’s plan would be like adding a floor on top of a crumbling tower block. England does not have too little social housing. It has too much: at 16% of the total stock, more than any other G7 country. In some London boroughs nearly 40% of homes are social housing. And far from being “the foundation for everything”, as Mr Burnham says, the council-housing system is wasteful, unfair and a barrier to growth.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，伯纳姆的方案就像是在一栋摇摇欲坠的楼房上再加一层。英格兰并非社会住房不足。它反而过剩：社会住房占全部住房存量的16%，在七国集团中高于任何其他成员国。一些伦敦自治市镇，近40%的住房是社会住房。并且，与伯纳姆所说的“万物之基”相去甚远，市政住房体系是浪费、失衡且阻碍增长的。

paragraphs.en: England has two types of housing support. First, tenants in social housing enjoy discounted rents. Second, anyone deemed needy after a means test, whether in a private home or a social one, can receive a rent subsidy (“housing benefit”). The Economist crudely calculates that discounted rents to the first group add up to an implicit subsidy of £20bn ($25bn) a year. For the two-thirds of social-housing tenants who are on welfare, this saving is mostly recycled within government, lowering the benefit bill needed to cover rent. But for the remaining one-third not on welfare, it’s a bonanza.
paragraphs.zh: 英格兰有两类住房支持。第一类是，居住在社会住房的租户可以享受低于市场的租金。第二类是，经过收入等资格审查后，被认定有需要的人，无论住在私人住房还是社会住房，都可以获得租金补贴（“住房补助”）。《经济学人》粗略估计，第一类住房的租金减免每年合计形成约200亿英镑（约250亿美元）的隐性补贴。对其中三分之二依赖福利的社会住房租户来说，这部分节省大多在政府体系内循环，减少了支付租金所需的补贴支出。而对剩下三分之一未领取福利的人来说，这就是一笔横财。

paragraphs.en: New social lets in Kensington and Chelsea were nearly 80% below market rents in 2024-25, a £32,000 annual saving per household. Social tenancies are often for life and can even be inherited. Once you’re in, there’s no more means-testing, regardless of how much your fortunes improve. Of the 4m households in social housing in England, more than 10% have incomes in the national top 40%. Some are rich. The wife of the president of Sierra Leone, who lives in a palace, was recently found to have a council home in London as well.
paragraphs.zh: 2024—25年，肯辛顿和切尔西（Kensington and Chelsea）的新分配社会住房租金几乎比市场租金低80%，每户每年可节省3.2万英镑。社会住房租约通常是终身居住权，甚至可以传承。只要你拿到以后，不论你的家庭状况后来如何改善，都不再进行任何资格收入审查。在英格兰约有400万户住在社会住房中的家庭中，超过10%家庭的收入位于全国前40%。其中一些家庭并不穷。塞拉利昂总统夫人——她住在一座宫殿里——近期也被发现拥有伦敦的一套议会住房（council home）。

paragraphs.en: Thus, some high earners enjoy ultra-cheap social housing, while more than 1m households on benefits rent from private landlords. In much of the country, housing benefits are stingy and rents are high, so this latter group is at risk of homelessness. A system that arbitrarily foists feast on some and famine on others is not just.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，一些高收入者能享受极低成本的社会住房，而超过100万户领取福利的家庭却要向私人房东租房。在全国许多地区，住房补贴偏低而房租又很高，这一后者群体因此面临无家可归的风险。一个制度如果随意地让部分人吃得太饱、让另一部分人挨饿，那就是不公正的。

paragraphs.en: It also slows economic growth by making people less mobile. If you have a lifelong council tenancy, you don’t want to lose it. So you are less likely to move elsewhere in search of a better job. And many of the council homes in city centres are occupied by pensioners, who are also frozen in place, blocking young jobseekers from moving in. (Some 60% of adults in social housing do not work.) Mr Burnham seems to think immobility is a virtue: he vows to build a country where “No one has to leave to get on in life.”
paragraphs.zh: 它还会通过降低人员流动性来拖慢经济增长。如果你拥有终身议会住房租约，你就不想失去它，于是你就不太可能为了更好的工作机会而搬去别处。再加上，许多市中心的议会住房由退休老人占据，他们也被“固定”在原地，这进一步阻挡了年轻求职者进驻。（大约60%的社会住房成年人不在工作。）伯纳姆似乎认为“不流动”是一种美德：他誓言要建设一个“没有人必须离开才能在生活中有所进步”的国家。

paragraphs.en: Rather than spending vast sums on building new council homes, Mr Burnham should instead raise all social rents to market rates. Then, those who truly need support could be given extra means-tested benefits to cover the difference. When the Tories tried and failed to introduce a similar scheme in 2015, opponents labelled it “social cleansing”. Yet the proposal is not to evict tenants, merely to charge them market rents. It would direct spending to people as and when they need it, instead of pinning it to a pile of bricks and mortar. Such a reform should be phased in gradually, to give people time to adjust. It would free up billions.
paragraphs.zh: 与其在建设大量新议会住房上投入巨额资金，伯纳姆应该改为把所有社会住房租金提高到市场水平。然后，真正需要帮助的人可以通过额外的、按收入核定的补贴来弥补租金差额。2015年保守党曾尝试实施类似方案却失败时，反对者将其称为“社会清洗”。然而这项提议并不是驱逐租户，而只是向他们收取市场租金。这样一来，支出将按人、按需求在需要时发放，而不是把钱“绑”在一堆砖瓦上。此类改革应当循序渐进地推进，给大家留出调整时间。它将释放出数十亿英镑的资金。

paragraphs.en: Fixing social housing would not solve all England’s housing woes. To do that, the country also needs to let builders put up more homes. Sir Keir Starmer enacted some reforms to make England’s planning system less insanely slow and obstructive. But other changes, such as stricter safety rules, gummed it up again. Fewer new homes were added last year than the year before. To pick up the pace, England needs a nationwide zoning system, in which it is harder to veto development, and looser quotas for “affordable housing” in private projects, which currently make many of them unprofitable.
paragraphs.zh: 解决保障性住房问题并不能治好英国所有的住房难题。要真正做到这一点，这个国家还需要让开发商建造更多房屋。基尔·斯塔默爵士推行了一些改革，旨在让英格兰的规划体系不再慢得离谱、堵得难以运转。但是，其他改革又把问题搞乱了，例如更严格的安全规定，让流程再次受阻。去年新增住房数量比前一年还少。要提速，英格兰需要一个全国性的分区制度，让开发项目更难被否决，并且在私人项目中放宽“可负担住房”配建比例的硬性要求，因为这些配套要求目前使得许多项目变得无利可图。

paragraphs.en: Mr Burnham has given no sign of entertaining such ideas. On the contrary, he has said that “undue weight” has been placed on planning reform. That’s a pity. For too long England has been failed by cuddly-sounding housing policies that are in fact toxic. Social housing is one example; another is the “green belt” restriction around cities, which chokes off housebuilding, even on wasteland. Our proposal to scrap sub-market rents is the opposite kind of policy. It sounds harsh, but would act like a tonic. ■
paragraphs.zh: 伯纳姆先生并没有表现出要认真考虑此类想法。相反，他甚至表示“对规划改革”被“过度强调”。这令人惋惜。英国长期以来一直被那些听起来温和、实则有害的住房政策所误导。保障性住房是一个例子；另一个是围绕城市的“绿带”限制，它扼杀了住宅建设，甚至连荒废土地都无法开发。我们提出取消低于市场租金政策，正是这种类型的相反思路。它听起来听似严厉，却会起到振奋针的作用。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订阅者可订阅我们的“观点”通讯，汇集最优质的社论、专栏、来稿和读者来信。
